<!--
Thank you for contributing to Marque.

Before opening:
- Read CONTRIBUTING.md and GOVERNANCE.md.
- Editorial PRs (typos, links) need no MIP.
- Normative PRs (MUST/SHOULD/MAY, wire format, identity, legal-proof, bridge rules)
  MUST be accompanied by a merged MIP. Do not open such a PR without a MIP number.
-->

## What kind of PR is this?

- [ ] Editorial (typo, link, grammar, formatting)
- [ ] Spec clarification (resolve ambiguity without changing intended behavior)
- [ ] Example, schema, or test vector
- [ ] MIP (draft, revision, or status transition)
- [ ] Normative change — references MIP #_____
- [ ] Other (explain)

## Summary

_One paragraph: what changes, and why?_

## Linked issue or MIP

- Closes #
- Implements MIP-

## Backwards compatibility

_If this PR modifies normative spec text, describe the impact on existing implementations. If none, write "no normative impact" with a one-sentence justification._

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] I have linked the relevant issue or MIP.
- [ ] If normative: the MIP is merged (at least in Draft status) and references this PR.
- [ ] Schemas and examples are updated in sync with any spec change.
- [ ] I have not used `--force` push after reviewers have engaged.
- [ ] Commit messages follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/) (see [CONTRIBUTING §Conventional Commits](../CONTRIBUTING.md#conventional-commits)).

## For reviewers

_Any areas where you specifically want scrutiny — risky subsections, novel claims, or places where you're unsure about the design choice._
